title: Bardan Monastery
tagline: Bardan Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Bardan Monastery or Bardan Gompa is a 17th-century Buddhist monastery, approximately 12 kilometres south of Padum, in Zanskar, Ladakh, northern India at the side of the Lungnak riv
overview.description: Bardan Monastery or Bardan Gompa is a 17th-century Buddhist monastery, approximately 12 kilometres south of Padum, in Zanskar, Ladakh, northern India at the side of the Lungnak river. The monastery also ran several smaller hermitages in the area. The monastery consists of a large Dukhang or assembly hall which has some grand statues of Buddhist figures and several small stupas.

topPlaces.name: Padum
topPlaces.description: Padum (also known as Padam), is a town and the administrative headquarter of the Zanskar district of Ladakh in India. Named after the Buddhist guru Padmasambhava, it was historically one of the two main capitals of the Zanskar Kingdom, the other being Zangla.

items.note: Padum (also known as Padam), is a town and the administrative headquarter of the Zanskar district of Ladakh in India. Named after the Buddhist guru Padmasambhava, it was historically one of the two main capitals of the Zanskar Kingdom, the other being Zangla.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bardan Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bardan Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bardan Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (102 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (188 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bardan Monastery?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bardan Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bardan Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Bardan Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
